modelsCompleteTable.name: Suggestion
modelsCompleteTable.cascades: N/A (no FK relations despite having foreign key fields)

modelsCompleteTable.name: BoardYear
modelsCompleteTable.cascades: N/A (no FK relations despite board/year fields)

missingCascadeDeletes.summary: 4 relations missing cascade deletes — will cause foreign-key violations on delete

highPriority.issue: NO cascade. Deleting a User with payments throws FK violation.
highPriority.impact: Admin cannot delete users with payment history. Requires manual cleanup first.

highPriority.issue: NO cascade. Deleting a ClassCategory referenced by MCQExamPackage fails.
highPriority.impact: Cannot delete class categories that have exam packages. Orphan risk on the reverse side.
highPriority.fix: Add onDelete: Cascade (or SetNull if package should survive class deletion)

highPriority.issue: NO cascade. Same issue as MCQExamPackage.classId.
highPriority.impact: Cannot delete class categories that have CQ exam packages.

highPriority.issue: NO cascade. Deleting an MCQ used in an exam set question fails.
highPriority.impact: Cannot clean up MCQs that are referenced by exam sets. Data integrity risk.
highPriority.fix: Add onDelete: Cascade (or handle manually before MCQ deletion)

highPriority.issue: NO cascade. Deleting a CQ used in an exam set question fails.
highPriority.impact: Cannot clean up CQs that are referenced by exam set questions.

missingIndexes.summary: 12 models lack indexes on commonly queried foreign key and filter fields

entries.why: Frequently queried: get all subjects for a class. Unique compound [slug, classId] does not help for classId-only queries.
Frequently queried: get all chapters for a subject. Unique compound [slug, subjectId] does not optimize subjectId-only lookups.
Frequently queried: get all topics for a chapter. Unique compound [slug, chapterId] does not optimize chapterId-only lookups.
Frequently queried: get all resources for a lecture.
Frequently queried: get all notes for a user, or notes on a specific content item. Zero indexes currently.
Frequently queried: get recently viewed items for a user. Zero indexes currently.
Frequently queried: filter exams by class, type, status. Zero indexes currently.
Frequently queried: get all questions for an exam.
Frequently queried: get results for a user or exam.
Frequently filtered by class, subject, or chapter. Zero indexes currently.
Frequently queried: list bundles by class or type.
Frequently queried: get all items in a bundle.

entries.issue: slug is NOT marked @unique. Can have duplicate slugs.
entries.risk: Two suggestions with same slug causes routing/display collisions.

missingUniqueConstraints.note: BoardYear has @@unique([board, year]) ✓. Payment has @@unique([userId, contentType, contentId, status]) — could block multiple pending payments of same type; review if intentional.

missingRelations.summary: 11 places where foreign-key fields lack @relation definitions, meaning no FK constraints at the database level

entries.issue: Fields exist as optional Strings but have NO @relation decorators. No FK constraints enforced.
entries.risk: Suggestion can reference non-existent class/subject/chapter. No cascade behavior.
entries.fix: Add @relation to ClassCategory, Subject, Chapter respectively (or make nullable @relation with onDelete: SetNull)

entries.issue: board and year are plain Strings with no relations to Board or ExamYear models.
entries.risk: BoardYear can reference board slugs that don't exist in Board table. No FK integrity.
entries.fix: Add @relation to Board and ExamYear models, or keep as-is if intentional loose coupling.

entries.issue: String? with no relation to User (admin).
entries.risk: Can reference non-existent admin user ID.

entries.issue: String? with no relation to User (admin).
entries.risk: Can reference non-existent admin user ID.

entries.issue: String? with no relation to User (admin).
entries.risk: Can reference non-existent admin user ID.

entries.issue: String? with no relation to User (admin).
entries.risk: Can reference non-existent admin user ID.

entries.issue: String? with no relation to Payment.
entries.risk: Can reference non-existent payment. No integrity check.

entries.issue: String? with no relation to Payment.
String? with no relation to Payment.

orphanRecordRisks.summary: Deleting parent records in several chains will either fail (no cascade) or leave orphan data with no cleanup path

chains.detail: Payment lacks cascade — deleting a user with payments throws FK error. Must nullify or delete payments first.
chains.fix: Add onDelete: Cascade to Payment.user relation, or add a cleanup step before user deletion

chains.detail: MCQExamPackage and CQExamPackage reference ClassCategory WITHOUT cascade. Deleting a ClassCategory with packages fails.
chains.fix: Add onDelete: Cascade to both relations, or restrict deletion if packages exist

chains.detail: MCQExamSetQuestion.mcqId has no cascade. Deleting an MCQ used in exam sets fails.
chains.fix: Add onDelete: Cascade, or require removing MCQ from all exam sets first

chains.detail: CQExamSetQuestion.cqId has no cascade. Deleting a CQ used in exam sets fails.
chains.fix: Add onDelete: Cascade, or require removing CQ from all exam sets first

chains.detail: Suggestion.classId has no relation at all (no FK). Deleting a ClassCategory leaves Suggestion records referencing a deleted class.
chains.fix: Either add @relation with SetNull or clean up suggestions referencing deleted class

chains.detail: BoardYear references boards/years by plain string — no FK. Deleting a board/year leaves dangling references in BoardYear.

cascadeChainClassCategory.description: Content hierarchy cascade chain is correctly configured end-to-end:
cascadeChainClassCategory.chain: ClassCategory → Subject (CASCADE) → Chapter (CASCADE) → Topic/Lecture/MCQ/CQ (all CASCADE) → Resource (CASCADE from Lecture), MCQExamSetQuestion (NO CASCADE from MCQ — BUG)

fieldTypeIssues.summary: 7 models use String fields to store structured JSON data — losing Postgres JSONB query capabilities and type safety

entries.why: Stores a JSON array of subject IDs as a string. Cannot use Postgres JSON operators (jsonb_path_query, @> etc.). Manual parsing required in application code.
Same issue as MCQExamPackage.subjectIds.
Stores JSON answer data as plain string. No query support, no validation.
Stores JSON map of {mcqId: answer} as string.
Comma-separated string pretending to be an array. Cannot query individual tags.
Same as MCQ.tags.
Comma-separated list of chapter IDs. Cannot query or join. Should be a proper ExamChapter junction table for referential integrity.
Stores JSON array [a,b,c,d] marks as string.
Stores JSON config for non-CQ question types as string.
Stores JSON annotation data as string.

entries.note: Field name 'showAnnotatedImages' has a typo: 'Annotated' should be 'Annotated' (correct: Annotated). Minor but could cause API inconsistency.

softDeleteAnalysis.summary: 0 models have deletedAt. 12 models lack isActive. No soft-delete capability anywhere in schema.

modelsMissingIsActive.name: User
modelsMissingIsActive.impact: Cannot disable/lock user accounts without deleting them entirely.

modelsMissingIsActive.name: ExamQuestion
modelsMissingIsActive.impact: Acceptable — child of Exam, cascade delete handles it.

modelsMissingIsActive.name: Progress
modelsMissingIsActive.impact: Can accumulate stale progress entries with no way to hide.

modelsMissingIsActive.name: Bookmark
modelsMissingIsActive.impact: Minor — users can delete bookmarks directly.

modelsMissingIsActive.name: Note
modelsMissingIsActive.impact: Minor — users manage their own notes.

modelsMissingIsActive.name: RecentlyViewed
modelsMissingIsActive.impact: Minor — old entries naturally age out.

modelsMissingIsActive.name: Notification
modelsMissingIsActive.impact: Cannot soft-delete notifications. Users see all.

modelsMissingIsActive.name: AuditLog
modelsMissingIsActive.impact: Acceptable — audit data should be append-only.

modelsMissingIsActive.name: SiteSetting
modelsMissingIsActive.impact: Cannot disable a setting, only delete it.

recommendations.action: Add 'isActive Boolean @default(true)' and optionally 'deletedAt DateTime?' for GDPR-style soft-delete.
Add optional 'deletedAt DateTime?' to allow cleanup of stale progress without data loss.
Add 'isActive Boolean @default(true)' or 'deletedAt DateTime?' so users can dismiss/delete notifications.
Add 'isActive Boolean @default(true)' to allow disabling settings without deleting them.

enumTypeAnalysis.summary: Only 1 Prisma enum (Role) is defined. 17+ string fields should be enums or DB-backed enum tables for type safety.
enumTypeAnalysis.dbDrivenValuesNote: If settings or content types need runtime modification without schema migrations, use a lookup table approach (reference table with seeded values + admin CRUD UI) instead of Prisma enums. ContentType model already serves this pattern. Consider using it for: Payment.method, Board.color palette, Navigation.location.

siteSettingModelReview.adequacy: ADEQUATE for basic key-value needs, LIMITED for complex configuration
strengths: Simple key-value store with @unique key constraint
Optional 'group' field enables logical setting grouping (seo, homepage, navigation)
Optional 'label' field provides human-readable names for admin UI

limitations.detail: Cannot natively store booleans, numbers, JSON objects, or arrays. Every value must be serialized/deserialized in application code. Booleans stored as 'true'/'false' strings with manual parsing.
limitations.fix: Use Json type instead of String to support multiple data types natively

limitations.detail: No field indicating what data type a setting value should be (string, number, boolean, json). Admin UI must guess or hardcode per-key rendering.
limitations.fix: Add enum/string field: type (string | number | boolean | json)

limitations.detail: Cannot disable/suspend a setting without deleting it. Deleting removes the record entirely with no recovery.
Only updatedAt is tracked. No creation audit trail for when settings were added.

limitations.issue: No validation or constraints on value
limitations.detail: Any string can be stored in value regardless of what the key expects. No min/max, pattern, or type validation at DB level.
limitations.fix: Validate at application layer; optionally add a JSON schema constraint

complexSettingsNeeds.useCase: Feature flags map (e.g., {newDashboard: true, aiRecommendations: false})
complexSettingsNeeds.limitation: Cannot store as flat string — requires JSON serialization
complexSettingsNeeds.workaround: Store JSON string in value, parse manually

complexSettingsNeeds.useCase: Homepage hero section configuration (title, subtitle, CTA button, background image)
complexSettingsNeeds.limitation: Structured object requires JSON string storage
complexSettingsNeeds.workaround: Store as JSON string in value field

complexSettingsNeeds.limitation: Complex nested data not suitable for flat key-value
complexSettingsNeeds.workaround: Use separate models (ContentPackage already exists for this)

siteSettingModelReview.overall: The SiteSetting model works for flat key-value settings (site name, meta description, API keys for external services, toggle flags). For complex structured configuration, consider migrating value to Json type or using dedicated models.

redundantIndexes.summary: 2 models have explicit @@index on fields that are already uniquely indexed

entries.why: supabaseUserId is already @unique — PostgreSQL automatically creates a unique B-tree index. Redundant.
email is already @unique — PostgreSQL already indexes it. Redundant.

denormalizedFields.summary: Fields that duplicate data from related models, risking inconsistency

entries.issue: MCQ stores classLevel and subjectId directly, even though it already links to Chapter (which links to Subject, which links to ClassCategory). These are denormalized for query performance.
entries.risk: classLevel/subjectId on MCQ could become inconsistent with the Chapter's actual class/subject.
entries.mitigation: Application must ensure consistency when creating/updating MCQs. Alternatively, derive from the Chapter → Subject → ClassCategory chain.

entries.issue: Same pattern as MCQ. Denormalized for performance.
Exam stores classLevel directly rather than deriving from related entities.

contentHierarchyIntegrity.summary: The core content hierarchy (ClassCategory → Subject → Chapter → Topic/Lecture/MCQ/CQ) has proper cascade chain but lacks some relational links
observations: MCQ and CQ store subjectId and classLevel directly (denormalized) but have NO @relation to Subject or ClassCategory — they only relate to Chapter.
There is no direct relationship from MCQ/CQ to Subject or ClassCategory at the Prisma level.
Exam references classLevel as a string, and optionally subjectId (no relation), and chapterIds as a comma-separated string (no relation).
The denormalized fields make it possible for MCQs/CQs to have classLevel or subjectId that doesn't match the Chapter's class/subject.
